description.value:
Used as a replacement for skills rolled with base COOL, EMP, or WILL

Used for rolling in checks which involve force of will, charisma, general intelligence, or intution. Note that if the check is specialized and the mook is not, the DV should be raised 1-3 tiers.

Used as a replacement for fighting or ranged weapon skills, excluding evasion. Note that this uses REF as a base for reasons involving initiative - set REF of the mook first, then consider that number when considering the Martial skill.
Used as a replacement for skills which rely on base Body or Dex, which are not covered by the Martial skill, and for Evasion.
Used for skills which rely on base INT or TECH.
A cybernetical replacement leg. Comes with a free foot.
Provides traction for climbing vertically. Must be paired.
Built-in hydraulics negates penalty for jumping. Must be paired.
A cybernetical replacement arm. Comes with a free hand.
A concealable Melee Weapon. Can't hold anything with this hand while activated.
Light version of an Armorjack. No armor penalty.
Light version of an Armorjack for your head. No armor penalty.
Each finger in your hand has a different tool. Great for Techs!

A revitalized bow, reloaded as part of an Attack Action.

+1 to Attack.
An artificial eye. Choose Kiroshi Optics!
Provides a +1 to aimed shots.
Plating on your hand which allows you to utilise Smartguns. Requires a neural link.
Turns weapon into a "Smartgun". Refer to all rules specified on pg. 344 before using.

Req Targeting Scope Cyberware, otherwise won't fire. If miss single attack by 4 or less, roll second attack at same DV 1d10+10 (not normal stat). Can add luck. Target can dodge the second roll as normal if able to do so.

Deals no damage. If meat target hit DV13 Resist Torture/Drugs. On fail falls Prone (see pg. 169) and unconscious for 1 minute. Can be woken by damage or another person using an action to touch/shake them.
Telescope in your eye up to 800m/yds. +1 to single shot/aimed shot if greater than 50m away.
Reduces penalties against targets in darkness, smoke/fog to 0.

information.notes:
Ariandel is a solo rockergirl who started her career as an Elflines cosplayer. She quickly found success to coincide with the rise of the game, elevated further as she was one of the first participants in the Zoo (a Biotechnica multi-month program for exotic bodysculpting - see Interface RED Vol. 2.)

Ariandel still plays the game, but has shifted her focus to mercwork, embracing her persona to become a cool, efficient and fleet-footed thief. More personally, she keeps a cool face during conventions (which she is often paid to attend), but has come to dislike the game's other players. Having taken her persona and made it her life, she's disillusioned with them, viewing them as wasting their lives and without ambition.

Ability - Elven Grace: Ariandel can dodge bullets using the physical skill, and speaks fluent Tolkein elvish, which is also used in Elflines. As an action, she can turn one person in a scene into her fan, and is a rank 5 rockerboy for the purposes of doing so.

An efficient huntress, Ariandel is capable of creating rope snares, tripwires, and other traps; the DV to evade or escape is 15.

If Befriended: Ariandel is both form and function; friends can spend a downtime afternoon with her (up to 2x a week) to earn 10 IP towards archery (or a single level if they have no levels in the skill, the first time they do so). Additionally, she'll offer fashion advice for costumes and characters, an essential skill for any edgerunner - Wardrobe and Style ranks cost 25% less to acquire if she's regularly consulted on the topic.
